data.body:
INVESTIGATION (2026-06-10, attempted, deferred): the three ACs split by coupling.

ACHIEVABLE STANDALONE:
- AC1 'no reconciler module >800 LOC' — applier.py (~3,480 LOC) can be split into seam-aligned siblings (outbound_apply / inbound_apply / apply_runtime / event_io; _build_leaves() ~:1396 is the registry seam) loaded via the SAME importlib pattern already in use. Move-only, reconciler unit suite as the gate.

COUPLED TO fare-rant-clasp (the repackage) — cannot be met without it:
- AC2 'zero importlib-by-path loads of acli' + 'delete all 5 _load_*_module shims'.
  Root cause verified in applier.py:37-39: the reconciler modules are spec-loaded BY PATH under test ('rebar_reconciler' is a test-package shadow), so normal cross-module imports between reconciler files do not resolve at test time. The five importlib-by-path loaders (mutation, _errors, adf, ticket_reducer, acli-integration) are the workaround for THAT, not only for acli-integration.py's hyphen. Renaming acli-integration.py -> rebar_reconciler/acli.py removes the hyphen reason but the test-shadow reason remains, so the loaders are still required. Worse: splitting applier into more siblings under the current scheme needs MORE importlib loaders, not fewer.
  => 'zero importlib-by-path loads' requires the engine to be a properly importable package first — i.e. fare-rant-clasp (Rec 5 / repackage). The acli rename also has a 37-file reference blast radius (engine + tests + dispatcher) that the repackage handles holistically.

RECOMMENDATION: do fare-rant-clasp first (it makes the package importable and removes the shadow), THEN this split lands cleanly and meets all three ACs in move-only commits. Or land AC1 (size split, importlib pattern retained) standalone now and re-scope AC2 onto fare-rant-clasp. Ticket left OPEN (claim released).
